case.title: KRONSBEIN v. TRUSTEES OF SCHOOLS OF TOWNSHIP THREE

opinion.h4: KRONSBEIN v. TRUSTEES OF SCHOOLS OF TOWNSHIP THREE, (1966)
KRONSBEIN v. TRUSTEES OF SCHOOLS OF TOWNSHIP THREE ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF ILLINOIS. No. 183. Decided October 10, 1966.
opinion.p: The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
opinion.h4: BOARD OF SATANTA JOINT RURAL HIGH v. GRANT CTY. PLAN., (1966)
opinion.p: BOARD OF SATANTA JOINT RURAL HIGH SCHOOL DISTRICT NO. 2 ET AL. v. GRANT COUNTY PLANNING BOARD ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF KANSAS. No. 192. Decided October 10, 1966.
195 Kan. 640, 408 P.2d 655, appeal dismissed and certiorari denied.
Robert C. Londerholm, Attorney General of Kansas, and J. Richard Foth, Assistant Attorney General, for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of jurisdiction. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for a writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
